title: Fix the Inefficient Array Index Finder

statement.markdown: Write a function that finds the index of the first occurrence of a given value in a sorted array. The function should return -1 if the value is not found. The input array is sorted in ascending order.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be an array of integers and a target value.
statement.outputFormat: The output should be the index of the first occurrence of the target value, or -1 if the value is not found.
statement.notes: The function should be efficient and handle large inputs.

constraints.description: The size of the input array

examples.explanation: The function should return the index of the first occurrence of the target value, which is 2.
The function should return -1 if the target value is not found.

hints.text: Use a binary search algorithm to find the target value efficiently.

editorial.title: Editorial: Efficient Array Index Finder
editorial.intuition: The problem requires finding the index of the first occurrence of a target value in a sorted array. A binary search algorithm can be used to achieve this efficiently.
editorial.bruteForce: A brute force approach would involve iterating through the array and checking each element until the target value is found, which would result in a time complexity of O(n).
editorial.betterSolution: A binary search algorithm can reduce the time complexity to O(log n) by dividing the search space in half at each step.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution would be to use a binary search algorithm with a while loop to find the target value, returning the index when found or -1 otherwise.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness involves showing that the binary search algorithm correctly finds the first occurrence of the target value in the sorted array.
commonMistakes: Not initializing the left and right pointers correctly
Not returning -1 when the target value is not found
